hold:storage holds a plurality of measurement records, each a directed entry with six fields: producing measurer id, target entity id, property id, numerical value, method id, source tag

combined.entityName: patent_claim_1
acquire:physical measurement apparatus acquires at least one measurement record by interaction with a physical target
output:apparatus outputs measurement record to storage
hold:storage holds a plurality of measurement records, each a directed entry with six fields: producing measurer id, target entity id, property id, numerical value, method id, source tag
record:source tag takes one of two values: first value indicates record produced by physical interaction; second value indicates record produced by computation

operate:storage operates on append-only basis: subsequent record with same composite key overwrites prior record in storage while prior record is preserved in append-only event log external to storage
group:convergence analyser groups records by (target, property) pair
compute:convergence analyser computes a relative-spread measure of numerical values for each group containing two or more records
classify:convergence analyser classifies each group as converged, tension, divergent, or single according to relative-spread measure against predetermined thresholds
compute:measurer-correlation analyser computes counts of each measurer's groups in each classification
classify:measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as systemic-crack source when two or more of its groups are divergent or tension
classify:measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as systemic-opening source when two or more of its groups are converged and none are divergent or tension
classify:measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as local-crack source when exactly one of its groups is divergent or tension

determine:population-decomposition analyser determines, for a (target, property) group classified as tension or divergent, whether distribution of numerical values admits decomposition into two or more components under a gaussian-mixture model selected by bic improvement threshold, subject to minimum-component-size and minimum-inter-component-separation guards
propose:when decomposition is found, analyser proposes split of target entity identifier into two or more sub-identifiers with each record assigned to exactly one sub-identifier
effect:proposed split is effected on storage through rewriting of the target-entity identifier field of each record
emit:feedback-signal generator emits a machine-readable feedback signal encoding the analyser's output for each measurer classification and each proposed identifier split

route:feedback-signal interface routes feedback signal to an actor configured to annotate the storage or to cause modification of how subsequent measurement records are acquired by the physical measurement apparatus

establish:source tag is the sole basis for invoking the echo-chamber classifier and the pattern-requirement rule, gating downstream logic that distinguishes physical-interaction from computational evidence

classify:physical-interaction records and computational records share the storage schema and are classified under the same grouping and relative-spread computations without reference to whether the producing measurer is a physical instrument or a computational measurer
gate:source tag alone gates downstream logic distinguishing physical-interaction from computational evidence, because it is the sole distinguishing field on each directed entry
exclude:system does not comprise an ontology layer assigning semantic types to entities, properties, or methods
exclude:system does not comprise a separate object-type or property-type registry external to the storage
exclude:system does not comprise a separate model-card, model-registry, or model-configuration record external to the storage for characterising a producing measurer
hold:any characterising attribute of a producing measurer held by the system is held as a directed entry in the storage under the same six-field schema as a measurement record of a physical target

perform:all semantic labelling, if performed, is performed external to the claimed system and is not consumed by the convergence analyser, the measurer-correlation analyser, or the population-decomposition analyser

result:because the system excludes all external ontology, registry, and model-card structures, any measurer characterisation must reside in the same storage schema as measurement records, making the schema self-contained and uniform
patent_claim_2
identify:among groups formed by the convergence analyser, a subset of groups in which at least two records are produced by distinct computational measurers bearing distinct method identifiers
detect:group contains at least one record produced by a physical measurement apparatus
detect:convergence analyser has classified the group as converged
classify:group is a confirmed echo chamber — convergence among distinct computational measurers is corroborated by physical measurement
detect:convergence analyser has classified the group as tension or divergent
classify:group is a refuted echo chamber — computational convergence is contradicted by physical measurement showing tension or divergence
detect:group contains no record produced by a physical measurement apparatus
classify:group is an untested echo chamber — cannot confirm or refute computational convergence without physical measurement
judge:confirmed-, refuted-, and untested-echo-chamber signals are each significant enough to warrant routing to the actor via the feedback-signal interface
record:prior refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to each measurer are accumulated over the event log
derive:a per-measurer trust weight can be derived from the accumulation of prior refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to that measurer
attenuate:a measurer's contribution to its own measurer classification is reduced in inverse proportion to its trust weight, because repeated prior refutations lower confidence in that measurer
judge:incorporating per-measurer trust weights derived from refutation history into the measurer-correlation analyser is a meaningful improvement to the system of claim 1
patent_claim_3
read:a time-ordered sequence of refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to each measurer is read from the append-only event log
compute:a decay-weighted aggregate of refuted-echo-chamber signals is computed over a configurable time window
emit:the aggregated value is emitted as the trust-weight argument optionally consumed by the measurer-correlation analyser
conclude:the per-measurer trust weight is the product of reading, decay-weighting, and emitting the aggregated refuted-echo-chamber signals
leads to:routing the trust-weight loop through the event log rather than through mutation of prior coupling entries results in the trust-weight loop operating via the append-only log
judge:it is architecturally significant that the trust-weight loop operates through the event log rather than by mutating prior coupling entries
patent_claim_4
detect:a (target, property) group exists in which all records carry the source tag indicating computation
determine:the group lacks any physical-interaction record
suppress:the converged classification is suppressed because all records are computational with no physical-interaction record present
classify:the group is classified as single instead of converged, caused by the suppression rule applied to all-computation groups
judge:a group lacking any physical-interaction record is architecturally incapable of achieving a converged classification — this incapability is treated as a necessary and desirable design property
patent_claim_5
partition:measurer's groups are partitioned by property identifier
identify:at least one property subset where a specified fraction of the measurer's groups are classified as divergent or tension, exceeding a first threshold
identify:at least one property subset where a specified fraction of the measurer's groups are classified as converged, falling below a second threshold distinct from the first
conclude:both a divergent-dominant subset and a converged-dominant subset exist for the same measurer simultaneously
emit:the co-existence of both subsets causes emission of a universal-co-divergence signal identifying the measurer together with the divergent and non-divergent property subsets
route:the universal-co-divergence signal is routed through the feedback-signal interface to the actor
judge:distinguishing divergent from converged property subsets within a single measurer is significant enough to warrant a dedicated signal and actor notification
patent_claim_6
record:a group contains at least one record produced by a physical measurement apparatus
record:a group contains at least one record produced by a computational measurer
measure:the relative-spread measure is computed using all records in the group regardless of source tag
classify:the group is classified under the four-valued classification using the relative-spread measure
detect:the group's classification is converged
detect:at least one physical-interaction record is present in the group
gate:admission of additional computational records is gated on continued presence of at least one physical-interaction record when classification is converged
detect:computational records form a majority in a group classified as converged
judge:a computational-record majority must not suppress admission of further physical-interaction records
detect:the last physical-interaction record is removed from a converged group
cause:removal of the last physical-interaction record causes re-classification of the group under the group-consists-entirely-of-computational-records rule of claim 4
patent_claim_7
define:the actor in claim 1 is being specified as one or more of several possible types
consume:a human operator receives the feedback signal through a human-machine interface

respond:the human operator modifies at least one of: sampling schedule, sensor-selection parameter, or data-gating parameter of the physical measurement apparatus, as a result of consuming the feedback signal
consume:a human operator receives the feedback signal (without a specified interface constraint)
respond:the human operator annotates the storage with an additional directed entry, as a result of consuming the feedback signal
consume:an automated control subsystem receives and acts on the feedback signal without any human intermediation
distinguish:the claim treats human-mediated and fully automated consumption of the feedback signal as legally distinct and independently claimable actor types
conclude:the actor role in claim 1 is intentionally broad, covering the full spectrum from direct human control to full automation, with the feedback signal as the common operative element
patent_claim_8
identify:a claim dependency — claim 8 depends on claim 7
observe:the actor is characterized as an automated control subsystem
observe:the automated control subsystem is configured to respond to a feedback signal
infer:the feedback signal originates from the system described in claim 7
cause:receipt of feedback signal triggers modification of the physical measurement apparatus operation
evaluate:the modification requires no human input — fully autonomous operation is asserted
observe:first modification option: altering a sampling schedule of the physical measurement apparatus
observe:second modification option: altering a sensor-selection or sensor-gain parameter
observe:third modification option: altering a data-gating parameter controlling admission of physical-interaction records into storage
infer:the three modification options are disjunctive — at least one must apply, not all are required
evaluate:the data-gating option extends control to the input boundary of the storage, not just the sensor layer
infer:claim 8 narrows claim 7 by specifying the actor type and constraining it to autonomous, feedback-driven apparatus modification
patent_claim_9
comprising:system includes a vocabulary store holding an operational-mechanism vocabulary with language-independent numerical identifiers
carries:each identifier carries a boolean admissibility flag with two possible values
indicating:first flag value indicates identifier names a physical or computational mechanism embodied by a measurement instrument
indicating:second flag value indicates identifier names a theoretical commitment about reality or an assumption about a measurement target
configured to reject:vocabulary store rejects at insertion time any identifier whose admissibility flag takes the second value
constrained to read:any consumer of the vocabulary store is constrained to read only identifiers whose flag takes the first value
comprising:storage holds a plurality of inference steps each comprising a claim descriptor, a convergence-status annotation, and a list of admissible operational-mechanism identifiers
selected from:convergence-status annotation is drawn from at least the values converged, tension, and divergent
drawn from:list of identifiers in each inference step is drawn from the vocabulary store
configured to process:inference-step consumer processes the plurality of inference steps under a reading discipline that excludes identifiers with second-value flags
enforces:vocabulary store enforces admissibility via schema-level validation making inadmissible identifiers architecturally incapable of entering the reading path
reflects:any inference drawn by the consumer from shared identifiers across distinct inference steps reflects shared instrument mechanism and not shared theoretical framing
patent_claim_10
assign:each inference step receives a marker taking one of at least two structurally distinguishable forms based on convergence-status annotation
distinguish:first form is assigned to steps annotated as converged
distinguish:second form is assigned to steps annotated as divergent or tension
propagate:markers are propagated along edges of a graph where nodes are admissible operational-mechanism identifiers and edges connect co-occurring identifiers within inference steps
decay:marker intensity diminishes by a per-hop decay factor as propagation proceeds along edges
detect:a collision event occurs at a shared operational-mechanism identifier where markers from two or more distinct inference steps coincide with accumulated intensity above a threshold
classify:collision is an opening-collision when all contributing markers are of the first form (converged)
classify:collision is a crack-collision when all contributing markers are of the second form (divergent or tension)
classify:collision is a bridge-collision when contributing markers include both first and second form markers
emit:a collision signal is emitted identifying the shared operational-mechanism identifier, the classification type, and the inference-step identifiers of contributing steps
patent_claim_11
bound:graph traversal is bounded to a fixed hop-count limit
terminate:propagation terminates when marker intensity falls below a specified decay threshold
result:bounding hop-count and applying decay threshold causes worst-case time complexity to be sub-quadratic
characterize:worst-case time complexity of collision detection is sub-quadratic in the number of operational-mechanism identifiers in the vocabulary store
patent_claim_12
holds:storage holds a plurality of measurer entities
drawn:measurer entities are drawn from the same identifier space as target entities
is representable:a measurer entity is representable as the target of an inference step produced by another measurer
is configured:marker-propagation analyser is configured to propagate markers through inference steps
appears:propagation occurs when a measurer appears as target in an inference step
requires:propagation proceeds without schema change to the storage
patent_claim_13
acquiring:physical measurement apparatus interacts with physical target to obtain at least one measurement record
storing:acquired record is stored together with further measurement records in a storage
comprising:each stored record is a directed entry with six fields: producing measurer identifier, target entity identifier, property identifier, numerical value, method identifier, and source tag
distinguishing:source tag takes one of two predetermined values to distinguish physical-interaction records from computation-produced records
constraining:storage is append-only with composite-key overwrite and an external append-only event log
grouping:records are grouped by (target, property) pair
computing:for each group with two or more records, a relative-spread measure of numerical values is computed
classifying:each group is classified as converged, tension, divergent, or single based on relative-spread measure
counting:for each measurer, its groups in each classification are counted
classifying:each measurer is classified as systemic-crack, systemic-opening, local-crack, or none based on group classification counts
testing:for each tension or divergent group, the distribution of numerical values is tested for gaussian-mixture decomposition into two or more components
applying:decomposition is subject to minimum-size, bic-improvement, and minimum-separation guards
proposing:when decomposition succeeds, a split of the target entity identifier is proposed
effecting:the proposed split is effected on the storage
emitting:a machine-readable feedback signal is emitted encoding each measurer classification and each proposed split
routing:feedback signal is routed to an actor configured to annotate storage or modify how subsequent records are acquired by the physical measurement apparatus
excluding:the method does not maintain an ontology layer assigning semantic types to entities, properties, or methods
excluding:the method does not maintain a separate object-type or property-type registry external to the storage
excluding:the method does not maintain a separate model-card, model-registry, or model-configuration record external to storage for characterising a producing measurer
constraining:any characterising attribute of a producing measurer is represented as a directed entry in storage under the same six-field schema as a measurement record of a physical target
patent_claim_14
hold:system maintains a vocabulary of language-independent numerical identifiers
carry:each identifier carries a boolean admissibility flag
distinguish:flag distinguishes instrument mechanism identifiers from theoretical commitment or target assumption identifiers
reject:system rejects at insertion time any identifier whose flag indicates theoretical commitment or target assumption
hold:system maintains a plurality of inference steps
comprise:each inference step contains a claim descriptor, a convergence-status annotation, and a list of admissible operational-mechanism identifiers
process:inference steps are processed under a reading discipline
admit:reading discipline does not admit any identifier flagged as theoretical commitment or target assumption
reflect:inference drawn from shared identifiers across distinct steps reflects shared instrument mechanism only
exclude:inference does not reflect shared theoretical framing
patent_claim_15
assign:each inference step receives a marker of one of at least two structurally distinguishable forms based on its convergence-status annotation
propagate:markers are propagated along edges of a graph whose nodes are admissible operational-mechanism identifiers, subject to a per-hop decay factor and a fixed hop-count limit
detect:a collision event is detected at any shared identifier where markers from two or more distinct inference steps coincide above a threshold
classify:the collision event is classified as an opening-collision, crack-collision, or bridge-collision according to the forms of the contributing markers
patent_claim_16
identifying:groups of (target, property) records that contain at least two records from distinct computational measurers with distinct method identifiers
identifying:among those groups, which contain at least one physical-interaction record and which do not
classifying:each such group as confirmed, refuted, or untested based on agreement of computational records with physical-interaction records when present
computing:a decay-weighted per-measurer trust weight derived from prior refuted classifications attributed to each measurer, using the event log
attenuating:each measurer's contribution to its own systemic classification is reduced by its trust weight
routing:a feedback signal encoding the classifications and attenuated systemic labels is sent to the actor
patent_claim_17
identify:a non-transitory computer-readable medium exists as the claimed subject matter
carry:the medium carries instructions
cause:execution of instructions by one or more processors causes the processors to perform a method
execute:one or more processors execute the carried instructions
perform:the processors perform the method of any one of claims 13, 14, 15, 16, or 16a
incorporate:claim 17 incorporates by reference the method steps of claims 13 through 16a as the operative functional scope
characterize:the medium claim is a beauregard-style claim providing medium-based patent coverage for the incorporated method claims
patent_claim_20
takes:source tag takes a third predetermined value distinct from the first two values
indicates:third value indicates the record was produced by an automated actor performing an action on the target entity
comprises:each record whose source tag carries the third value further comprises one or more structured pointers
identifies:each structured pointer identifies a coupling entry in storage whose content was read by the automated actor
conditioned:the action was conditioned upon the coupling entry content read prior to producing the action
records:storage records action records in the same six-field schema as records with first or second source tag values
patent_claim_21
comprises:a structured pointer contains a composite coupling key identifying a (measurer, target, property) triple in storage
identifies:the composite coupling key points to a specific triple in the storage
comprises:a structured pointer contains an event identifier identifying a specific event in the append-only event log
identifies:the event identifier points to a specific event in the append-only log from claim 1
comprises:a structured pointer contains a content-hash value
computed by applying:the content-hash value is produced by applying a cryptographic hash function to a canonical serialization of the coupling entry
resulted from:the canonical serialization reflects the storage state that resulted from the specific event identified by the event identifier
configured:the system is configured to respond to a subsequent retrieval operation referencing a structured pointer
recompute:upon retrieval, the system recomputes the content-hash from the storage state at the event identified by the event identifier
compare:the system compares the recomputed hash value against the stored content-hash value to verify integrity
patent_claim_22
comprising:system of claim 21 further comprises a loop-gain analyzer
identify:loop-gain analyzer identifies cyclic subgraphs from a directed graph induced by storage records and structured pointers
comprising:each cyclic subgraph comprises at least one record with source tag carrying third predetermined value
comprising:each cyclic subgraph comprises at least one record with source tag carrying one of first two predetermined values
compute:loop-gain analyzer computes spectral-radius metric from linearization of subgraph dynamics near current state for each cyclic subgraph
meets or exceeds:spectral-radius metric is evaluated against a predetermined threshold
emit:loop-gain analyzer emits typed cycle annotation stored in append-only event log for each subgraph whose spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds threshold
patent_claim_23
comprising:system of claim 22 includes a classifier component
configured to emit:classifier is designed to produce a dynamical-signature annotation for each qualifying cyclic subgraph
meets or exceeds:typed cycle annotation indicates spectral-radius metric reaches predetermined threshold
taking:dynamical-signature annotation takes one of at least four predetermined values
distinguishing:the four values distinguish between four distinct dynamical signatures
characterized by:first signature: measurement records converge toward value predicted by prediction records
characterized by:second signature: measurement records drift toward boundary values of property range
characterized by:third signature: target entity is altered in response to action records
characterized by:fourth signature: action-record selection is optimized against prediction records
patent_claim_24
receive:second classifier receives a coupling group of records whose source tag carries the third predetermined value and whose structured pointers reference a hypothesis inference step
represent:said records represent an adoption of the hypothesis inference step by automated actors
analyze:second classifier analyzes dynamics of convergence of measurement records against values predicted by the hypothesis inference step, over the coupling group and subsequent records in storage
emit:second classifier emits a mode annotation taking one of at least four predetermined values
characterize:first mode is characterized by absence of systematic convergence or divergence
characterize:second mode is characterized by convergence of measurements toward hypothesis predictions with direction of causation from measurement to hypothesis
characterize:third mode is characterized by convergence of measurements toward hypothesis predictions with direction of causation from hypothesis to subsequent measurement
characterize:fourth mode is characterized by divergence of measurements away from hypothesis predictions subsequent to a period of convergence
patent_claim_25
receive:system receives empirical-distribution characterization of measurement values for a target entity from its stored history
select:system selects one uncertainty-propagation method from a plurality based on the empirical-distribution characterization received
assume:first method assumes approximately gaussian distributions of measurement error
characterize:second method is robust to heavy-tailed or multi-modal distributions as distinct from gaussian case
record:selection of uncertainty-propagation method is recorded in the append-only event log as a typed annotation
patent_claim_26
compute:system computes empirical entropy over a rolling window of measurement values for a target entity
compute:system computes declared-variety value as a cardinality-derived function of the automated actor's most recent structured pointer set
compare:system compares empirical entropy against declared-variety value
exceed:empirical entropy exceeds declared-variety value by a predetermined margin, triggering subsequent actions
emit:system emits a requisite-variety-deficit event in the append-only event log
suspend:system suspends production of further action records from the automated actor referencing the target entity
maintain:system maintains suspension until pointer set is updated or a manual-override event is committed by an authorized operator
patent_claim_27
accommodates:storage holds typed-absence annotation for each target entity when no measurement record exists for a specific property
distinguishes:the typed-absence annotation takes one of at least four predetermined values distinguishing absences by their origin within a predetermined taxonomy
accommodates:storage holds actionability tier annotation for each target entity taking one of a predetermined plurality of values
indicates:actionability tier annotation indicates the degree to which the target entity is available for action records referencing it
configured:analyzers of claims 1, 22, 24, and 25 are configured to respect typed-absence annotations in their emissions
configured:analyzers of claims 1, 22, 24, and 25 are configured to respect actionability-tier annotations in their emissions
respect:analyzer emissions must honor both typed-absence and actionability-tier annotations as constraints on output
patent_claim_28
compute:decomposition of inference-step dependency graph into strongly-connected components prior to marker propagation
construct:condensation graph formed by contracting each strongly-connected component to a single node, yielding a directed acyclic graph
propagate:markers propagated on the condensation graph rather than directly on the original inference-step dependency graph
route:internal dynamics of each strongly-connected component containing more than one node routed to a fixed-point solver
compute:fixed-point solver computes performatively-stable states on each multi-node component under predetermined contraction conditions
patent_claim_29
meets or exceeds:cyclic subgraph's spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds the predetermined threshold
receive:counterfactual generator receives a set of historical action records comprising the cyclic subgraph
compute:gradient value computed as partial derivative of spectral-radius metric with respect to parameters of each historical action record
obtained:gradient value obtained via adjoint computation traversing the cyclic subgraph
select:perturbation set selected as those action records with largest absolute gradient magnitude, capped at predetermined maximum number
construct:one or more counterfactual histories constructed by replacing each perturbation-set action record with an alternative action record
rank:counterfactual histories ranked by reduction in spectral-radius metric when substituted for actual history
patent_claim_30
receive:system receives a coupling group that has been classified as divergent or under tension by the convergence analyzer
classify:convergence analyzer has previously classified the coupling group as divergent or under tension
enumerate:experimental-design generator enumerates a plurality of candidate measurement actions
specify:each candidate measurement action is specified by a proposing automated actor's structured pointer set, a target entity identifier, a property identifier, and a predicted uncertainty
compute:predicted uncertainty for each candidate measurement action is computed via the uncertainty-propagation layer
derive:expected-information-gain value is derived from the reduction in posterior uncertainty over the coupling group's property value
result:reduction in posterior uncertainty would result if the candidate measurement action were performed and yielded a measurement record with the predicted uncertainty
emit:generator emits a ranked list of candidate measurement actions sorted by expected-information-gain value
patent_claim_31
emits:ranked list is emitted by counterfactual generator or experimental-design generator
annotates:actionability-tier annotation exists as defined in claim 27
filters:ranked list is further filtered by actionability-tier annotation
references:candidate action records or candidate counterfactual histories reference target entities
indicates:actionability-tier annotation indicates non-actionable status for certain target entities
removes:candidate records referencing non-actionable entities are removed from ranked list
routes:candidate records referencing non-actionable entities are routed to human-review queue
substitutes:human-review queue substituted in lieu of emission to automated-action pathway
patent_claim_32
comprising:system includes claim 29 and claim 30 as components
comprising:system further includes a routing component
configured:routing component is configured to perform specific operations
receive:routing component receives a detected crack or detected cyclic subgraph from convergence analyzer of claim 1 or loop-gain analyzer of claim 22
determine:routing component determines dispatch target based on presence or absence of dynamical-signature annotation of claim 23
dispatch:detected crack or cyclic subgraph is dispatched to counterfactual generator of claim 29, experimental-design generator of claim 30, or both in parallel, depending on determination
record:routing component records a typed dispatch event in the append-only event log of claim 1
specifying:dispatch event specifies the detected crack or cyclic subgraph, the dispatch decision, and the selected generator or generators
patent_claim_33
indicating:third value indicates record was produced by an automated actor
performing:automated actor performs an action on the target entity
comprises:producing each action record comprises recording in the same six-field schema
recording:schema is the same as records whose source tag carries first or second values
identifying:one or more structured pointers identifying coupling entries whose content was read by the automated actor
read:automated actor read coupling entries prior to producing the action
conditioned:action was conditioned upon the coupling entries that were read
patent_claim_34
identifying:identifying cyclic subgraphs from a directed graph induced by records and structured pointers
comprising:inferring that each cyclic subgraph contains at least one record with a third predetermined source tag value
comprising:inferring that each cyclic subgraph contains at least one record with one of the first two predetermined source tag values
computing:computing a spectral-radius metric from linearization of subgraph dynamics near current state for each cyclic subgraph
meets or exceeds:evaluating whether a cyclic subgraph's spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds a predetermined threshold
emitting:emitting a typed cycle annotation for each cyclic subgraph whose spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds the threshold
patent_claim_35
stores:a non-transitory computer-readable medium stores instructions
executed:instructions are executed by one or more processors
cause:execution causes the processors to perform a method
perform:processors perform the method of claims 13, 33, and 34
